desc_ar: إنجليزي بسيط عن المال والميزانية: كيف نوفّر، نصرف، ونخطّط للمصروف في محل صغير.
why_ar: لتتعلّم كلمات الإنجليزي التي تصف المال، السعر، والتوفير في العمل والحياة اليومية.
outcomes: تقرأ نصاً بسيطاً عن الميزانية والمال
تتعلّم كلمات: save, spend, budget, price
warmup: هل تحب توفّر أو تصرف فلوسك؟

grammar.ar: الأسماء المعدودة وغير المعدودة + much / many
grammar.explain_ar: نستخدم many مع الأسماء المعدودة (نقدر نعدّها: customer/customers، shop/shops). ونستخدم much مع الأسماء غير المعدودة (ما نقدر نعدّها: money، time). ونقدر نستخدم some و a lot of مع النوعين.
grammar.explain_en_html:
Countable / Uncountable + much / many

We use many with things we can count (many customers, many shops).
We use much with things we cannot count (much money, much time).
Use some and a lot of with both.
The shop has many customers, but not much money.
grammar.formula: many + countable (customers) · much + uncountable (money) · some / a lot of + both

examples.sentence: The shop has many customers today.
Omar does not have much money this month.
examples.translation_ar: عمر ما عنده فلوس كثيرة هذا الشهر.

examples.sentence: We sell a lot of coffee every day.

mistakes.explanation_ar: customers معدودة، فنستخدم many مو much.
money غير معدودة، فنستخدم much مو many.

items.question: We don't have ___ time before the shop opens.

readings.label: A
paragraphs: Omar has a small *shop* in the city. He sells tea, coffee, and sweets. Every month, Omar makes a *budget*. A budget is a simple plan for money. He writes how much money comes in from the shop. Then he writes how much money goes out. Rent, tea, coffee, and bags all *cost* money. Omar wants to know the total *cost* before the month starts.

Omar likes to *save* money. He does not *spend* it all. He checks the *price* of every thing he buys. Some cups are *cheap*, and some cups are *expensive*. Omar buys the cheap cups for his customers. He looks for a good *deal* on coffee too. When the *price* is high, he waits. At the end of the month, Omar has some money left in the box. He puts it in the bank. A good budget helps him *save* a little money every month. Slowly, his shop grows.

choices: A man makes a budget for his shop
questions.correct_answer: A man makes a budget for his shop

questions.question_en: What does Omar sell in his shop?
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنه يبيع الشاي والقهوة والحلويات.
cheap تعني رخيص، أي سعره منخفض.

questions.question_en: What does Omar do with the money left at the end of the month?
questions.question_ar: ماذا يفعل عمر بالمال المتبقي في نهاية الشهر؟
choices: He puts it in the bank
questions.correct_answer: He puts it in the bank
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنه يضع المال المتبقي في البنك.

questions.question_en: Why does Omar buy the cheap cups?
questions.explanation_ar: يشتري الرخيص عشان التكلفة تبقى منخفضة.

questions.question_en: What kind of person is Omar with money?
questions.question_ar: أي نوع من الأشخاص هو عمر مع المال؟
questions.explanation_ar: نفهم من النص إنه حريص، يخطط ويوفّر.

questions.question_en: What does Omar do first when he makes a budget?
questions.question_ar: ماذا يفعل عمر أولاً عندما يعمل الميزانية؟
choices: He writes how much money comes in
questions.correct_answer: He writes how much money comes in
questions.explanation_ar: أول شيء يكتب كم فلوس تدخل، ثم كم تخرج.

vocab: a place where you buy things
He makes a budget every month.
the money you pay for something
to keep money and not spend it
to use money to buy things
He does not spend it all.
He checks the price of the cups.
He looks for a good deal.

readings.label: B
paragraphs: Khalid works in a big *market*. He gets his money at the end of the week. Khalid wants to *buy* a new bike. A good bike is *expensive*, so he must *save* money. His friend Sami likes to *spend* money fast. Sami buys food, games, and drinks every day.

Khalid has a plan. He does not *waste* money on small things. He brings food from home. He does not buy many drinks. Every week, he puts some money in a small box. This is his *savings*. After two months, Khalid has enough money. He goes to the *market* and finds a good *deal* on a bike. The *price* is low this week. Khalid is happy because his plan worked. Now Sami wants to *save* money too. He asks Khalid for help. Khalid smiles and says, 'A little money every week is a big help.'

questions.question_en: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Khalid saves money to buy a bike
A trip to the market fails
questions.correct_answer: Khalid saves money to buy a bike
questions.explanation_ar: النص عن خالد الذي يوفّر ليشتري دراجة.
قال النص إنه يعمل في سوق كبير.

questions.question_en: What does the word 'waste' mean here?
choices: To use money in a bad way
questions.correct_answer: To use money in a bad way
questions.explanation_ar: waste تعني يهدر المال، أي يستخدمه بطريقة سيئة.

questions.question_ar: لماذا يجب أن يوفّر خالد المال؟
choices: Because a good bike is expensive
questions.correct_answer: Because a good bike is expensive

questions.question_en: How does Khalid not waste money?
questions.explanation_ar: يجيب أكله من البيت وما يشتري مشروبات كثيرة.

choices: Khalid has enough money for a bike
questions.correct_answer: Khalid has enough money for a bike
questions.explanation_ar: بعد شهرين صار عنده فلوس كافية للدراجة.

questions.question_en: What can we learn from Khalid?
choices: Saving a little every week helps a lot
questions.correct_answer: Saving a little every week helps a lot
questions.explanation_ar: نتعلّم أن توفير القليل كل أسبوع يساعد كثيراً.

He wants to buy a bike.
to keep money and not spend it
to use money to buy things
to use money in a bad way

He earns money at the market.

listening.transcript: Hi, I'm Nasser. I have a small shop. Every month, I make a budget for my money. I don't spend much money on new things. I save some money every week. When the price is cheap, I buy more coffee. I have many customers, but not much money yet. My budget helps me a lot.

exercises.explanation_ar: قال ناصر إنه عنده محل صغير.

exercises.question_en: What does Nasser make every month?
How many customers does Nasser have?
When does Nasser buy more coffee?
exercises.explanation_ar: يشتري قهوة أكثر لما السعر رخيص.

exercises.question_en: Does Nasser spend a lot of money on new things?
exercises.explanation_ar: قال إنه ما يصرف فلوس كثيرة على أشياء جديدة.

exercises.question_en: Why is the budget good for Nasser?
exercises.explanation_ar: الميزانية تساعده كثير في تنظيم فلوسه.

writing.prompt_en: Write about how you save money. What do you save for? How do you not spend too much?
writing.prompt_ar: اكتب عن كيف توفّر فلوسك. لأي شيء توفّر؟ كيف ما تصرف كثير؟
hints: I do not spend much money on...

speaking.prompt_en: Talk about money. Do you like to save or spend? What do you want to buy?
speaking.prompt_ar: تحدث عن الفلوس. تحب توفّر أو تصرف؟ وش تبي تشتري؟
prep: حضّر كلمات: save, spend, budget, price
رتّب: هل توفّر أو تصرف، وليش
